title: peyritschia tolucensis
side_effects: Peyritschia tolucensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can lead to more severe symptoms, including abdominal pain, dizziness, and even liver damage. Furthermore, improper use of the plant may also cause allergic reactions, including skin irritation and respiratory problems, in some individuals. It is essential to exercise caution and consult with a medical professional before using any plant-based remedies to avoid these potential side effects.
